name: Chili Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 10 fluid ounces beef broth, or chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the beans using your preferred method: Add 10 cups hot water per pound of California dry beans and up to 2 teaspoons of salt per pound of beans (optional). Heat until boiling, then let boil for 2-3 minutes. Remove from heat, cover, and soak for at least 1 hour, preferably 4 hours or more.
2. Drain the beans and add them to a large pot with 6-8 cups of boiling water. Stir in garlic salt, onion salt, thyme, and marjoram. Cover and simmer until the beans are tender (2½ to 3 hours). Add hot water as needed to prevent the beans from drying out.
3. Use 3 cups of the cooked beans for another use later. Mash the remaining beans with their liquid.
4. Add the canned tomatoes, chili seasoning mix, and 1 cup of hot water. Heat until flavors are well blended (at least 10 minutes).
